Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\\n28\\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\\n29\\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -\u003e Response {\\n30\\t    let raw = uri.path();\\n31\\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\\n32\\t    let asset_path = raw\\n33\\t        .strip_prefix(\\\"/ui/\\\")\\n34\\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\\\"/ui\\\"))\\n35\\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\\n36\\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\\n37\\t        \\\"index.html\\\"\\n38\\t    } else {\\n39\\t        asset_path\\n40\\t    };\\n41\\t\\n42\\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\\n43\\t}\\n44\\t\\n45\\tfn serve_asset(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Response {\\n46\\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\\n47\\t        Some(content) =\u003e {\\n48\\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\\n49\\t            Response::builder()\\n50\\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\\n51\\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\\n52\\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\\n53\\t        }\\n54\\t        None =\u003e {\\n55\\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\\n56\\t            match UiAssets::get(\\\"index.html\\\") {\\n57\\t                Some(index) =\u003e Response::builder()\\n58\\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \\\"text/html; charset=utf-8\\\")\\n59\\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\\n60\\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\\n61\\t                None =\u003e StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\\n62\\t            }\\n63\\t        }\\n64\\t    }\\n65\\t}\\n66\\t\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs\",\"content\":\"//! {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. 1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"encoding/json"
     5		"os"
     6		"path/filepath"
     7		"testing"
     8		"time"
     9	
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
    11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    13	)
    14	
    15	type cleanupCommandJSON struct {
    16		DryRun                      bool             `json:"dry_run"`
    17		ProjectRoot                 string           `json:"project_root"`
    18		TempRoot                    string           `json:"temp_root"`
    19		ScannedTempDirs             int              `json:"scanned_temp_dirs"`
    20		ScannedEvidenceDirs         int              `json:"scanned_evidence_dirs"`
    21		CompleteEvidenceDirs        int              `json:"complete_evidence_dirs"`
    22		RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs int64            `json:"removed_unregistered_temp_dirs"`
    23		RemovedRegisteredWorktrees  int64            `json:"removed_registered_worktrees"`
    24		RemovedRunStateFiles        int64            `json:"removed_run_state_files"`
    25		BytesReclaimed              int64            `json:"bytes_reclaimed"`
    26		InodesReclaimed             int64            `json:"inodes_reclaimed"`
    27		Warnings                    []map[string]any `json:"warnings"`
    28		BlockedErrors               []map[string]any `json:"blocked_errors"`
    29		Observations                []map[string]any `json:"observations"`
    30	}
    31	
    32	func setupCleanupCommandProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
    33		t.Helper()
    34		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
    35		tempRoot := t.TempDir()
    36		t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", tempRoot)
    37		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"), 0o755))
    38		return projectRoot, tempRoot
    39	}
    40	
    41	func writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t *testing.T, root, name, projectRoot, attemptID string) string {
    42		t.Helper()
    43		path := filepath.Join(root, name)
    44		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(path, 0o755))
    45		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(path, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
    46			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
    47			BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
    48			AttemptID:    attemptID,
    49			WorktreePath: path,
    50		}))
    51		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(path, "scratch.txt"), []byte("stale\n"), 0o644))
    52		return path
    53	}
    54	
    55	func TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting(t *testing.T) {
    56		projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
    57		stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-dryrun-20260506T154739-deadbeef", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-deadbeef")
    58		runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
    59		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
    60			BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
    61			AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-feedface",
    62			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
    63			WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
    64		}))
    65	
    66		root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
    67		out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup")
    68		require.NoError(t, err)
    69	
    70		assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: would remove 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
    71		assert.Contains(t, out, "run again with --apply")
    72		assert.DirExists(t, stalePath)
    73		assert.FileExists(t, runStatePath)
    74	}
    75	
    76	func TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources(t *testing.T) {
    77		projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
    78		stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-apply-20260506T154739-feedface", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-feedface")
    79		runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
    80		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
    81			BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
    82			AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-cafebabe",
    83			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
    84			WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
    85		}))
    86		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt-complete")
    87		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
    88		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
    89		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
    90	
    91		root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
    92		out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
    93		require.NoError(t, err)
    94	
    95		assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
    96		assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
    97		assert.NoFileExists(t, runStatePath)
    98		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
    99		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
   100	}
   101	
   102	func TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape(t *testing.T) {
   103		projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
   104		writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-json-20260506T154739-12345678", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-12345678")
   105		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
   106			BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
   107			AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-90abcdef",
   108			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
   109			WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
   110		}))
   111	
   112		root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
   113		out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--json")
   114		require.NoError(t, err)
   115	
   116		var report cleanupCommandJSON
   117		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
   118	
   119		assert.True(t, report.DryRun)
   120		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs)
   121		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedRunStateFiles)
   122		assert.NotEmpty(t, report.Warnings)
   123		assert.NotNil(t, report.BlockedErrors)
   124		assert.NotNil(t, report.Observations)
   125		assert.Greater(t, report.BytesReclaimed, int64(0))
   126		assert.Greater(t, report.InodesReclaimed, int64(0))
   127	}
   128	
   129	func TestCleanupCommand_DoesNotRemovePreservedEvidence(t *testing.T) {
   130		projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
   131		stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-preserved-20260506T154739-abcdef12", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-abcdef12")
   132		preservedPath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-preserved-20260506T154739-34567890", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-34567890")
   133		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(preservedPath, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
   134			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
   135			BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
   136			AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-34567890",
   137			WorktreePath: preservedPath,
   138			Preserved:    true,
   139		}))
   140		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt-complete")
   141		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
   142		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
   143		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
   144		runsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "runs", "run-complete")
   145		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(runsDir, 0o755))
   146		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"), []byte(`{"run_id":"run-complete"}`), 0o644))
   147	
   148		root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
   149		out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
   150		require.NoError(t, err)
   151	
   152		assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 0 run-state file(s)")
   153		assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: preserved 2 complete evidence bundle(s)")
   154		assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
   155		assert.DirExists(t, preservedPath)
   156		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
   157		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
   158		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"))
   159	}

    18	
    19	type tryHookRunnerStub struct {
    20		t            *testing.T
    21		promptSource []string
    22	}
    23	
    24	type blockingTryExecutor struct {
    25		started chan struct{}
    26		once    sync.Once
    27	}
    28	
    29	func (e *blockingTryExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
    30		e.once.Do(func() { close(e.started) })
    31		<-ctx.Done()
    32		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
    33			BeadID:    beadID,
    34			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
    35			Detail:    ctx.Err().Error(),
    36			BaseRev:   "feedface",
    37			ResultRev: "feedface",
    38		}, ctx.Err()
    39	}
    40	
    41	func runInterruptedTryCommand(t *testing.T) (*bead.Store, string, error) {
    42		t.Helper()
    43	
    44		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
    45		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
    46		env.CreateDefaultConfig()
    47		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
    48		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
    49		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
    50			ID:    "try-interrupt-001",
    51			Title: "Interrupted try bead",
    52		}))
    53	
    54		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
    55		runner := &blockingTryExecutor{started: make(chan struct{})}
    56		factory.tryExecutorOverride = runner
    57		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
    58	
    59		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    60		root.SetContext(ctx)
    61	
    62		var buf bytes.Buffer
    63		root.SetOut(&buf)
    64		root.SetErr(&buf)
    65		root.SetArgs([]string{"try", "try-interrupt-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing"})
    66	
    67		done := make(chan error, 1)
    68		go func() {
    69			done <- root.Execute()
    70		}()
    71	
    72		select {
    73		case <-runner.started:
    74		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
    75			t.Fatal("try executor did not start")
    76		}
    77		cancel()
    78	
    79		var err error
    80		select {
    81		case err = <-done:
    82		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
    83			t.Fatal("try command did not return after cancel")
    84		}
    85	
    86		return store, buf.String(), err
    87	}
    88	
    89	func (r *tryHookRunnerStub) Run(opts agent.RunArgs) (*agent.Result, error) {
    90		r.promptSource = append(r.promptSource, opts.PromptSource)
    91		switch opts.PromptSource {
    92		case "bead-lifecycle-lint":
    93			return &agent.Result{
    94				ExitCode: 0,
    95				Output:   `{"score":9,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
    96			}, nil
    97		case "bead-lifecycle-triage":
    98			return &agent.Result{
    99				ExitCode: 0,
   100				Output:   `{"classification":"transport","recommended_action":"retry","rationale":"transient","suggested_amendments":"none","suggested_followup_beads":[]}`,
   101			}, nil
   102		default:
   103			r.t.Fatalf("unexpected prompt source: %q", opts.PromptSource)
   104			return nil, nil
   105		}
   106	}
   107	
   108	// TestTry_BeadNotFound verifies that "ddx try unknown-id" exits non-zero
   109	// and writes "bead not found: <id>" to stderr.
   110	func TestTry_BeadNotFound(t *testing.T) {
   111		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
   112		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
   113	
   114		out, err := executeCommand(root, "try", "unknown-bead-xyz")

   117			"stderr must contain the documented 'bead not found' message")
   118	}

   193	// TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes verifies the core AC: given a ready bead
   194	// and a stub executor that returns success, the command claims the bead,
   195	// invokes the executor, and exits zero.
   196	func TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes(t *testing.T) {
   197		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
   198		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
   199		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
   200		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
   201			ID:    "happy-bead-001",
   202			Title: "Happy path bead",
   203		}))
   204	
   205		var executorCalled bool
   206		stubExecutor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
   207			executorCalled = true
   208			assert.Equal(t, "happy-bead-001", beadID, "executor must receive the targeted bead ID")
   209			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
   210				BeadID:    beadID,
   211				Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
   212				ResultRev: "deadbeef01234567", // non-empty so ClosureGate passes
   213			}, nil
   214		})
   215	
   216		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
   217		factory.tryExecutorOverride = stubExecutor
   218		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
   219	
   220		out, err := executeCommand(root, "try", "happy-bead-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
   221		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx try with a successful stub executor must exit zero: %s", out)
   222		assert.True(t, executorCalled, "executor must have been called")
   223	
   224		// Verify the bead was claimed (status should be closed after success).
   225		b, storeErr := store.Get("happy-bead-001")
   226		require.NoError(t, storeErr)
   227		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "bead must be closed after successful execution")
   228	}
   229	
   230	// TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent verifies that ddx try records the
   231	// bead-hint routing intent evidence and prints the concise routing-intent
   232	// line when a durable tier label is present.
   233	func TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent(t *testing.T) {
   234		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)

   236		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillPath, 0o755))
   237		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(skillPath+"/SKILL.md", []byte("lint"), 0o644))
   238	
   239		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
   240		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
   241		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
   242			ID:          "hint-bead-001",

   244			Labels:      []string{"tier:smart"},
   245			Description: "PROBLEM\nhard decision\n\nSMART JUSTIFICATION:\nThis bead decides the durable execution-hint contract.\n",
   246		}))
   247	
   248		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
   249		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
   250		factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {

Existing unit tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go:52-100 verify the loop stops after one executor call, but cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 only routes into the worker and there is no end-to-end test that exercises the command boundary with two ready beads and an injected resource_exhausted setup failure. That leaves a gap where a future change could accidentally claim the second bead after ENOSPC-like failure.\\n\\nROOT CAUSE\\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:451-556 owns startup cleanup, pre-claim cleanup, and the main loop exit path. cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 hands ddx work straight into that loop, but no test currently proves the observable command behavior: exit after the first resource_exhausted attempt, no second claim, and the failed bead remains reclaimable.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go or a new sibling test file: add a command-level test that seeds two ready beads in a temp project and injects a resource checker / cleanup runner failure that returns resource_exhausted before the second claim.\\n- Assert the first bead is touched once, the second bead is never claimed, and the loop emits the resource_exhausted stop message.\\n- Verify the first bead stays open and unowned so it can be reclaimed later.\\n- Keep the harness in temp dirs and fakes; do not consume real disk or inodes.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- Do not add new production behavior to the loop itself.\\n- Do not test unrelated provider/model routing, retry cooldowns, or no_changes paths.\\n\\nACCEPTANCE\\n1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim exists and runs ddx work against two ready beads with an injected resource_exhausted setup failure.\\n2. The test proves only the first bead is touched and the second bead is not claimed.\\n3. The test verifies the command emits the resource_exhausted stop reason and leaves the failed bead open and reclaimable.\\n4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim\" -count=1 passes.\\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.'"

ID:       ddx-d3aca2b7
Title:    test: cover task execution cleanup and resource exhaustion end-to-end
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress

Desc:     PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.
Accept:   1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim runs ddx work against two ready beads with injected resource_exhausted setup failure and verifies only the first bead is touched.
2. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees creates real stale DDx worktrees/dirs under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR and verifies ddx work or ddx cleanup prunes them safely.
3. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable verifies ddx try on a target bead fails with resource_exhausted, exits non-zero, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed without execute-loop-retry-after.
4. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence verifies complete .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* are not removed.
5. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter verifies periodic cleanup can be driven deterministically with fake time and does not run concurrent destructive passes.
6. The e2e tests use temp directories and injectable fakes rather than consuming real disk/inodes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd|TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestCleanupEndToEnd|TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd" -count=1 passes.
